assertions.title: Submitter resources reconcile imported registrants and missing-member skips

steps.action: Run apply for round 10815 and inspect resource reconciliation plus skipped-artifact resource entries.
steps.expected: Resolvable eligible registrants should appear as submitter resources while missing members appear only in the skipped artifact.
steps.observed: Apply completed with `targetEligibleRegistrants=828` resolvable members and emitted 8 `missing-member` resource entries; together those reconciled to the 836 eligible legacy registrants. The run continued past missing member 2058745 instead of aborting.

steps.action: Check current API summary against the archived round-10815 footprint.
steps.expected: The imported resource surface should remain populated and contain no example data.
steps.observed: Current API summary still showed 828 resource rows for challenge 10815, matching the archived footprint. AGENTS.md documents one pre-existing duplicate submitter row in this shared fixture and instructs validators not to treat it as an importer regression.

assertions.title: Imported participant identities stay consistent across APIs

steps.action: Run the archived round-10815 legacy/API reconciliation for resources and submissions.
steps.expected: The same memberId should not be split across conflicting handles or cross-API identities.
steps.observed: The reconciliation reported `cross-API handle conflicts=0` and `split handles across memberIds=0` for round 10815.

evidence.network: Resource API and submissions API snapshots were compared against legacy identity mappings.

assertions.title: Challenge-level submission import reconciles imported and missing-member volume

steps.action: Inspect round-10815 apply submission reconciliation output and skipped artifact.
steps.expected: Imported submissions plus missing-member skipped submissions should reconcile to legacy non-example submission volume and distinct submitter volume.
steps.observed: Apply reported `legacyNonExampleSubmissions=1445`, `importedSubmissions=1444`, `missingMemberSkippedSubmissions=1`, `importedDistinctSubmitters=266`, and `missingMemberDistinctSubmitters=1`, which reconciled exactly to the legacy round totals.

assertions.title: Mixed-history members keep every non-example submission and exclude examples

steps.action: Run the archived Marinov member-specific timestamp reconciliation.
steps.expected: Member 22664170 should have all 27 non-example submissions, exclude 55 example runs, and preserve the latest non-example timestamp.
steps.observed: Marinov imported count was 27, imported timestamps overlapped only legacy non-example timestamps, and the max imported timestamp matched `1180539064719`; none of the 55 example runs appeared in imported submissions.

evidence.network: Submission API output was compared to read-only Informix legacy rows for member 22664170.

assertions.title: Whole-round submission identity and per-member distribution reconcile through skips

steps.action: Run the archived whole-round submission identity reconciliation for 10815.
steps.expected: Imported submission legacy ids plus skipped missing-member submission identities should match legacy one-for-one and preserve per-member counts.
steps.observed: The reconciliation reported `importedPlusSkippedEqualsLegacy=true`, `importedUnionSkippedEqualsLegacy=true`, `importedUnionSkipped submitter set=true`, and `resolvableMismatchCount=0`.

evidence.network: Submission API identities were reconciled against legacy long_submission rows and skipped-artifact entries.

assertions.title: Participant reruns preserve stable imported identities and skipped-member reporting

steps.action: Compare the first and second round-10815 apply runs and their skipped artifacts.
steps.expected: Imported submission identities and resource tuple sets should remain stable across reruns with deterministic skipped-member records.
steps.observed: Rerun stability checks returned true for the submission legacy-id set, resource tuple set, and canonicalized skipped-artifact records/reason codes. The documented shared-fixture duplicate submitter row did not change the tuple set.

evidence.network: Apply CLI plus API/legacy diffing across reruns.

assertions.title: Missing-member participant skips suppress the member's full participant footprint

steps.action: Inspect missing-member participant entries in the round-10815 skipped artifact and compare to imported surfaces.
steps.expected: Missing members should create no resource or submission footprint while resolvable members continue importing normally.
steps.observed: The skipped artifact recorded missing members including 2058745 and 22669623 with affected surfaces covering `resource` and `submission`; imported resources/submissions reconciled fully for the remaining resolvable members.

evidence.network: Resource and submission API snapshots were checked against skipped-artifact identities.

assertions.title: Participant skip artifacts are actionable and distinguish missing members

steps.action: Inspect the skipped artifact schema and representative participant skip records for 10815.
steps.expected: Each missing-member participant skip should include the round id, member id, reason, and affected participant surfaces.
steps.observed: The artifact used `reasonCode=missing-member` and included `legacyRoundId`, `memberId`, `memberHandle`, `affectedSurfaces`, and per-surface counts; per-submission skips also preserved `legacySubmissionId` for later manual reconciliation.

frictions.description: The shared dev fixture for round 10815 includes a documented pre-existing duplicate submitter row and reports challenge status ACTIVE instead of COMPLETED.
frictions.resolution: Applied the AGENTS.md higher-precedence instruction not to treat this shared-fixture baseline as an importer regression and validated tuple-set / imported-plus-skipped reconciliation instead.

summary: Validated all 8 participant assertions for round 10815: all passed. Evidence showed imported-plus-skipped reconciliation to legacy registration/submission totals, Marinov's full non-example history preservation, cross-API identity consistency, rerun-stable submission/resource identities, and actionable missing-member participant artifacts.
